plan: Reduce overall leverage by selling all leveraged ETFs.
Cut semiconductor exposure significantly.
Increase allocation to income-generating assets.
Trim broad index exposure to reduce concentration.
Add a high-quality software name for stability.
Maintain a larger cash buffer for safety.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with lower expected monthly returns. Risk is managed by cutting leverage and diversifying away from concentrated tech/semis.

plan: Slightly reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim semiconductor holdings to lower concentration.
Reduce broad index allocations to meet concentration limits.
Add a high-quality software stock for growth.
Introduce a Bitcoin-related tech name for diversification.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced approach aims for moderate growth with a focus on risk reduction. Expect steady returns with controlled drawdowns. Leverage remains modest.

plan: Reduce broad index exposure to free up capital.
Exit income holdings to focus on growth.
Increase semiconductor exposure, including leveraged ETFs.
Add top-rated software and semiconductor names.
Utilize leverage to amplify potential gains.
Maintain a small cash buffer for rapid deployment.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan seeks high growth by increasing leverage and concentrating in strong tech/semiconductor setups. Expect significantly higher potential upside but also much larger potential drawdowns and volatility due to leverage and daily rebalancing of leveraged ETFs.

plan: Eliminate all leveraged ETFs and volatile semiconductor exposure.
Significantly reduce high-beta software holdings.
Maximize allocation to income-focused ETFs.
Increase broad index exposure for stability.
Add energy sector exposure for income diversification.
Maintain a substantial cash buffer.
income.read: This income-focused plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and capital preservation. Expect low volatility and reliable, though variable, income. NAV can still decline in market downturns.
